message:
feat(robot): implement rate limit backoff with exponential growth (ntm-1ir2)

Add BackoffManager for rate-limited agents with exponential backoff:
- Base backoff: 30 seconds
- Multiplier: 2^n per consecutive rate limit
- Maximum cap: 5 minutes
- Per-session backoff tracking via global registry

Integration functions:
- CheckSendAllowed(session, paneID) - check if send is blocked
- RecordAgentRateLimit(session, paneID) - record rate limit hit
- ClearAgentBackoff(session, paneID) - clear after recovery

Includes comprehensive test suite (10 tests) covering:
- Basic backoff behavior
- Exponential escalation
- Maximum cap enforcement
- Backoff clearing and expiration
- Global registry management

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

 {"id":"ntm-1l9t","title":"Implement scrollback capture for checkpoints","description":"## Overview
Capture tmux pane scrollback as part of checkpoint for complete conversation history.

## Location
Create `internal/checkpoint/scrollback.go`

## Scrollback Capture
```go
type ScrollbackCapture struct {
    PaneID      string
    Lines       int
    Content     string
    Compressed  []byte
    Size        int64
}

func CaptureScrollback(session, paneID string, lines int) (*ScrollbackCapture, error) {
    // Use tmux capture-pane
    cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "capture-pane",
        "-t", fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", session, paneID),
        "-p",      // Print to stdout
        "-S", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", lines), // Start N lines back
        "-E", "-1", // End at current line
    )
    
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // Compress with gzip
    compressed := gzipCompress(output)
    
    return &ScrollbackCapture{
        PaneID:     paneID,
        Lines:      lines,
        Content:    string(output),
        Compressed: compressed,
        Size:       int64(len(compressed)),
    }, nil
}
```

## Storage
```
checkpoint-dir/
  checkpoint.json
  scrollback/
    cc_1.txt.gz
    cc_2.txt.gz
    user_pane.txt.gz
```

## Configuration
```toml
[checkpoints]
scrollback_lines = 5000      # Default lines to capture
scrollback_compress = true   # Always gzip
scrollback_max_size_mb = 10  # Skip if too large
```

## Considerations
- Large scrollbacks can be slow to capture
- Compression is essential for storage
- Some panes may have very little scrollback
- Binary output (rare) should be handled

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tmux capture-pane integration
- [ ] Gzip compression
- [ ] Configurable line limit
- [ ] Size limit handling
- [ ] Per-pane scrollback files
- [ ] Integration with checkpoint capture
- [ ] Tests with mock tmux","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:22.399393405-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:22.399393405-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-1l9t","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ox6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:36.660897518-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-1l9t","depends_on_id":"ntm-59ce","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:36.729542342-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-1m1m","title":"Implement file conflict detection","description":"Detect when multiple agents may have modified same files. Essential for parallel agent coordination.

DETECTION APPROACH:

1. GIT-BASED DETECTION:
   - Run `git status --porcelain` to get modified files
   - For each modified file, get modification time via `stat`
   - Correlate modification times with agent activity windows
   - If file modified during window when 2+ agents were GENERATING: flag as conflict

2. NEWLY CREATED FILES:
   - Track files created during session (not in initial git status)
   - If same file path touched by multiple agents: definite conflict
   - Use inotify/fsnotify for real-time tracking (optional enhancement)

3. AGENT MAIL INTEGRATION:
   - Query Agent Mail file reservations
   - If file reserved by Agent A but modified while Agent B active: flag
   - Confidence boost: reservation + activity = high confidence

CONFLICT CLASSIFICATION:
```go
type FileConflict struct {
    Path         string
    LikelyModifiers []string  // pane IDs
    GitStatus    string       // M, A, D, ??
    Confidence   float64      // 0.0-1.0
    Reason       string       // "concurrent_activity", "reservation_violation"
}
```

CONFIDENCE LEVELS:
- 0.9+: High confidence (reservation violation, overlapping edits)
- 0.7-0.9: Medium confidence (concurrent activity window)
- 0.5-0.7: Low confidence (possibly unrelated)

ACTIVITY WINDOW TRACKING:
- For each pane, track: last_activity_start, last_activity_end
- Activity = state was GENERATING for continuous period
- Overlap = two panes' windows intersect

LIMITATIONS:
- Cannot know exactly which agent wrote which bytes
- Best effort heuristic based on timing
- False positives possible with fast sequential edits

LOCATION: internal/robot/synthesis.go (new file)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Git status correctly parsed
- File modification times captured
- Activity windows tracked per pane
- Conflicts flagged with confidence scores
- Agent Mail reservations considered
- Handles newly created files","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:02:11.583632957-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T20:44:27.585260439-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-1m1m","depends_on_id":"ntm-sp0c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:02:28.624392209-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-97c5","title":"EPIC: IDE Integration (VS Code)","description":"# IDE Integration (VS Code Extension)

## Background & Motivation

Most developers work primarily in VS Code. IDE integration reduces context switching and enables unique value that terminal-only can't provide.

## CRITICAL: Focus on Unique IDE Value

The terminal TUI is already excellent. Don't rebuild it in VS Code.

**Unique IDE value:**
1. **Editor context injection**: Send selected code/current file to agents
2. **Quick access**: Status bar for at-a-glance info
3. **File decorations**: Show lock status in explorer

**NOT prioritized:**
- Full dashboard webview (duplicates TUI)
- Complex session management (use terminal)

## What the Extension Provides

### 1. Status Bar (High Value, Low Effort)
```
[NTM: myproject ● 4 agents]
```
- Click to open command palette
- Shows session name, agent count
- Color indicates status (green=ok, yellow=warning)

### 2. Commands (High Value, Medium Effort)
```
NTM: Send Selection to Agent
NTM: Send Current File to Agent
NTM: Spawn Agents for Project
NTM: Show Session Status
NTM: Open Terminal (attach to session)
```

### 3. Context Injection (Killer Feature)
```typescript
// When user invokes "Send Selection"
const selection = editor.selection;
const text = editor.document.getText(selection);
const filePath = editor.document.fileName;

// Construct context-aware prompt
const prompt = `
File: ${filePath}
Selected code:
\`\`\`
${text}
\`\`\`

[User's instruction here]
`;

// Send via ntm send
await ntm.send(session, target, prompt);
```

### 4. File Decorations (Medium Value, Medium Effort)
Show lock status in file explorer:
- 🔒 icon for locked files
- Tooltip: "Locked by GreenCastle (cc_1)"
- Requires Agent Mail integration

### 5. Simple Session View (Optional)
Activity bar panel with:
- List of sessions
- Agent count per session
- Quick actions (attach, kill)

NOT a full dashboard - just navigation.

## What We're NOT Building

- ❌ Full webview dashboard (use terminal TUI)
- ❌ Inline agent chat (complex, scope creep)
- ❌ Custom editor for prompts (overkill)

## Technical Approach

### CLI Wrapper
```typescript
class NtmClient {
    async getStatus(): Promise<RobotStatus> {
        const result = await exec('ntm --robot-status');
        return JSON.parse(result.stdout);
    }
    
    async send(session: string, target: string, prompt: string): Promise<void> {
        await exec(`ntm send ${session} ${target} ${quote(prompt)}`);
    }
}
```

### Extension Activation
Activate when:
- Workspace contains .ntm/ directory
- User runs NTM command
- NTM status bar clicked

## Priority Order

1. **P2**: Status bar + basic commands (NTM: Spawn, Send)
2. **P2**: Context injection (Send Selection)
3. **P3**: File decorations (requires Agent Mail)
4. **P3**: Session tree view
5. **P4**: Webview (if ever)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Status bar shows current session
- [ ] Commands in palette for common actions
- [ ] "Send Selection to Agent" works with context
- [ ] Extension published to marketplace
- [ ] Minimal dependencies, fast activation
- [ ] Degrades gracefully if ntm not installed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:28:29.775574-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:53:13.976369-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T23:53:13.976369-05:00","close_reason":"Feature-complete: Status bar, commands, context injection, file decorations, session tree view all implemented. Marketplace publishing requires manual steps with vsce."}

 {"id":"ntm-9ab0","title":"Implement variable substitution","description":"Substitute variables in prompts and parse step outputs. Enables dynamic workflows.

VARIABLE TYPES:

1. USER VARIABLES (${vars.X}):
   - Provided at runtime: --var name=value
   - From variable file: --var-file vars.yaml
   - Default values from workflow definition

2. STEP OUTPUTS (${steps.X.output}):
   - Raw captured output from step
   - Trimmed (leading/trailing whitespace removed)
   
3. STEP METADATA (${steps.X.field}):
   - .pane: pane ID used
   - .duration: execution time
   - .status: completed/failed/skipped
   - .agent: agent type used

4. ENVIRONMENT (${env.X}):
   - From environment variables
   - Fail if not set (unless default provided)

5. CONTEXT:
   - ${session}: session name
   - ${timestamp}: current ISO8601 time
   - ${run_id}: pipeline run identifier
   - ${workflow}: workflow name

OUTPUT PARSING (Computed Variables):
```yaml
- id: get_count
  prompt: Return the count as a JSON object
  output_var: data
  output_parse: json  # parse as JSON
  
- id: use_count
  prompt: Process ${vars.data.count} items  # access JSON field!
```

PARSING MODES:
- none: raw string (default)
- json: parse as JSON, access fields with dot notation
- yaml: parse as YAML
- lines: split into array by newlines
- first_line: first line only (trim rest)
- regex: extract with named groups

REGEX PARSING:
```yaml
output_parse: 
  type: regex
  pattern: "Count: (?P<count>\\d+)"
  
# Access: ${vars.result.count}
```

DEFAULT VALUES:
```yaml
vars:
  env:
    default: "development"
    
# Usage with fallback
${vars.undefined_var | "fallback_value"}
```

ESCAPING:
To use literal ${}, escape with backslash: \${not.a.var}

IMPLEMENTATION:
```go
func (s *Substitutor) Substitute(template string, state *ExecutionState) (string, error) {
    // Find all ${...} patterns
    pattern := regexp.MustCompile(`\$\{([^}]+)\}`)
    
    return pattern.ReplaceAllStringFunc(template, func(match string) string {
        varPath := extractPath(match)
        value, err := s.resolveVar(varPath, state)
        if err != nil {
            // Check for default: ${var | "default"}
            if default := extractDefault(match); default != "" {
                return default
            }
            return match // leave unsubstituted, will error later
        }
        return fmt.Sprint(value)
    })
}

func (s *Substitutor) resolveVar(path string, state *ExecutionState) (interface{}, error) {
    parts := strings.Split(path, ".")
    
    switch parts[0] {
    case "vars":
        return state.Variables[parts[1:]]
    case "steps":
        return state.Steps[parts[1]].Get(parts[2:])
    case "env":
        return os.Getenv(parts[1]), nil
    // ... other types
    }
}
```

LOCATION: internal/pipeline/variables.go (new file)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All variable types resolve correctly
- JSON/YAML parsing works with field access
- Regex parsing with named groups works
- Default values applied
- Escaping works
- Clear errors for undefined variables
- Nested field access works (${vars.data.nested.field})","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:04:09.579445224-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:05:40.132454546-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:05:40.132454546-06:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive variable substitution system in internal/pipeline/variables.go with: all variable types (vars, steps, env, context, loop), nested field access, default values with fallback syntax, escaping support, JSON/YAML/regex/lines output parsing. Updated executor.go to use new Substitutor. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-9ab0","depends_on_id":"ntm-gcgx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:04:31.570805043-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-c2b","title":"EPIC: Plugin & Extension System","description":"Allow users to extend NTM with custom agents, commands, and hooks.

## Problem Statement

NTM currently supports three hardcoded agent types: Claude, Codex, Gemini.
Users may want to use other agents or customize behavior:

1. **Other AI Agents**: Cursor, Aider, Continue, OpenAI API, local LLMs
2. **Custom Workflows**: Project-specific commands and automation
3. **Integration Hooks**: Run scripts before/after NTM operations
4. **Team Customization**: Share agent configs across a team

Currently, users must fork NTM to add new agent types or modify behavior.

## Goals

1. Allow custom agent definitions via config files
2. Support custom commands as executable scripts
3. Provide hook system for pre/post command execution
4. Make plugins discoverable and easy to install
5. Document plugin development clearly

## Why This Matters

Extensibility enables:
- Immediate support for any new AI coding agent
- Project-specific workflows without code changes
- Team-shared configurations and standards
- Community plugin ecosystem
- Future-proofing against agent landscape changes

## Success Criteria

- Users can define custom agents in ~/.config/ntm/agents/
- ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2 (custom agent)
- Users can add commands via ~/.config/ntm/commands/
- ntm my-custom-command (runs script)
- Hooks fire at defined extension points
- ntm plugins list shows installed plugins

## Plugin Types

### 1. Agent Plugins (~/.config/ntm/agents/*.toml)

Define new agent types:

# cursor.toml
[agent]
name = "cursor"
alias = "cur"
command = "cursor --headless --ai-mode"
description = "Cursor AI editor"
default_tags = ["cursor", "editor"]

Usage: ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2

### 2. Command Plugins (~/.config/ntm/commands/*)

Executable scripts become commands:

#\!/bin/bash
# ~/.config/ntm/commands/sync-all
# Description: Sync all agents with latest code

for session in $(ntm list --json | jq -r '.[].name'); do
  ntm send "$session" --all "git pull && review changes"
done

Usage: ntm sync-all

### 3. Hook Plugins (~/.config/ntm/hooks/*)

Run before/after NTM commands:

# hooks.toml
[hooks.pre_send]
command = "~/.config/ntm/hooks/log-prompt.sh"
pass_args = true

[hooks.post_spawn]
command = "notify-send 'NTM' 'Session created'"

## Technical Approach

Plugin Discovery:
- Scan known directories on startup
- Cache plugin metadata for performance
- Reload on ntm plugins refresh

Agent Plugin Loading:
- Parse TOML to AgentConfig struct
- Register with spawn/add commands
- Generate shell integration aliases

Command Plugin Execution:
- Discover executables in commands/
- Run with NTM context in environment
- Pass JSON via stdin, capture stdout/stderr

Hook Execution:
- Define extension points in each command
- Run hooks synchronously (pre) or async (post)
- Pass command context as JSON
- Fail-safe: hook errors don't break NTM

## Security Considerations

- Plugins are user-installed, user-trusted
- No automatic plugin download/installation
- Clear documentation of execution context
- Hooks can see all command arguments
- Consider --no-hooks flag for sensitive operations

## Interaction with Other Features

- JSON Output: Plugin commands can use --json
- Config: Plugins extend config schema
- Remote: Plugins work locally, commands sent to remote
- Tags: Custom agents can have default tags

## Future Possibilities

- Plugin registry/marketplace
- Version pinning for plugins
- Plugin dependencies
- Programmatic plugin API (Go plugins)

--robot-snapshot includes health data, but sometimes I just need a quick health check without the full state. This is the lightweight "are things working" query.

## Output Structure

```json
        "0.3": {"responsive": false, "issue": "no_output_5min"}
      }
    }
  },
  "alerts": ["0.3: no output 5min"]
}
```

## Health Heuristics
- responsive: has pane produced output recently AND is tmux pane alive
- output_rate: high (>1 line/sec), medium, low (<1 line/min), none
- last_activity_sec: time since last output line

## Alert Conditions
- No output for 5 minutes (configurable)
- Pane process exited
- High CPU in pane (if detectable)
- Disk space low
- Session unexpectedly killed

2. Detect stuck agents for restart

-{"id":"ntm-caib","title":"Implement alerting system","description":"Notify users when agent health states change. Focuses on human-oriented alerting.

## Revised Scope (v2)

After discussion, Agent Mail integration is **out of scope** for this task because:
1. Agents already know how to use Agent Mail via MCP (documented in AGENTS.md)
2. If an agent is healthy enough to receive mail, it can send its own alerts
3. For crashes, the agent can't receive mail anyway - desktop/webhook is more appropriate
4. Adding Agent Mail writing to ntm creates coupling we don't need

## Alert Channels (In Scope)

### 1. DESKTOP NOTIFICATIONS
- Linux: notify-send
- macOS: osascript (display notification)
- Windows: PowerShell toast (future)
- Include: session, pane, state, action suggestion

### 2. WEBHOOKS
- POST to configured URL(s)
- JSON payload with full event details
- Retry with backoff on failure
- Support multiple webhook URLs

### 3. STRUCTURED LOG
- Always log to stderr with JSON
- For integration with log aggregators
- Enables downstream processing

## Out of Scope

- **Agent Mail writing**: Agents handle their own inter-agent communication
- Agents can send Agent Mail when they detect issues (they already know how)
- If we need cross-agent notification of health events, that's a future feature

## Alert Events

- State transitions: healthy→degraded, degraded→unhealthy
- Rate limiting detected
- Restart triggered (include context loss warning)
- Restart failed
- Max restarts exceeded

## Debouncing

Prevent alert spam on flapping agents:
- Min interval between same alert: 60s
- Aggregate repeated alerts in window
- Track alert history per pane

## Configuration

```toml
[alerts]
enabled = true
debounce_seconds = 60

# Desktop notifications
desktop_notify = true
desktop_urgency = "normal"  # low, normal, critical

# Webhooks (array)
[[alerts.webhooks]]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/..."
events = ["unhealthy", "restart_failed"]

# Which events trigger alerts
alert_on = ["unhealthy", "rate_limited", "restart", "restart_failed"]
```

## Future Consideration

If we find strong need for ntm-to-Agent-Mail alerts, we can add:
- A webhook that POSTs to an Agent Mail bridge
- A config option to specify Agent Mail recipient
- But this is NOT required for v1

## Location

internal/robot/alerts.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- Desktop notifications work on Linux and macOS
- Webhooks fire correctly with retry
- Debouncing prevents spam
- All configured events trigger alerts
- Alert content is actionable","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:00:32.426745084-06:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:27:04.700397-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-caib","depends_on_id":"ntm-v5if","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:00:52.369396145-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-ceb","title":"Create internal/agentmail HTTP client package","description":"# Create internal/agentmail HTTP Client Package

## Purpose

Foundation for all Agent Mail integration. This package provides a clean Go interface to the MCP Agent Mail HTTP API.

## Technical Details

### Package Structure
```
internal/agentmail/
├── client.go       # HTTP client with connection pooling, retries
├── types.go        # Go structs matching Agent Mail data model
├── tools.go        # Methods for each MCP tool (send_message, fetch_inbox, etc.)
├── resources.go    # Methods for MCP resources (resource://inbox, etc.)
├── errors.go       # Error types with Agent Mail error codes
└── client_test.go  # Unit tests with mock server
```

### Client Configuration
```go
type Client struct {
    baseURL     string       // Default: http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/
    bearerToken string       // From AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN or config
    httpClient  *http.Client // With timeouts, connection pooling
    projectKey  string       // Cached project path
}

func NewClient(opts ...Option) (*Client, error)
func (c *Client) IsAvailable() bool  // Health check
```

### Key Types (matching Agent Mail schema)
```go
type Agent struct {
    ID              int       `json:"id"`
    Name            string    `json:"name"`            // e.g., "GreenCastle"
    Program         string    `json:"program"`         // e.g., "claude-code"
    Model           string    `json:"model"`           // e.g., "opus-4.1"
    TaskDescription string    `json:"task_description"`
    LastActiveTS    time.Time `json:"last_active_ts"`
}

type Message struct {
    ID          int       `json:"id"`
    ThreadID    string    `json:"thread_id"`
    Subject     string    `json:"subject"`
    BodyMD      string    `json:"body_md"`
    From        string    `json:"from"`
    To          []string  `json:"to"`
    Importance  string    `json:"importance"`  // normal, high, urgent
    AckRequired bool      `json:"ack_required"`
    CreatedTS   time.Time `json:"created_ts"`
}

type FileReservation struct {
    ID          int       `json:"id"`
    PathPattern string    `json:"path_pattern"`
    AgentName   string    `json:"agent_name"`
    Exclusive   bool      `json:"exclusive"`
    Reason      string    `json:"reason"`
    ExpiresTS   time.Time `json:"expires_ts"`
}
```

### JSON-RPC 2.0 Protocol
Agent Mail uses JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP:
```go
type JSONRPCRequest struct {
    JSONRPC string      `json:"jsonrpc"` // "2.0"
    ID      interface{} `json:"id"`
    Method  string      `json:"method"`  // "tools/call"
    Params  struct {
        Name      string                 `json:"name"`
        Arguments map[string]interface{} `json:"arguments"`
    } `json:"params"`
}

type JSONRPCResponse struct {
    JSONRPC string          `json:"jsonrpc"`
    ID      interface{}     `json:"id"`
    Result  json.RawMessage `json:"result,omitempty"`
    Error   *JSONRPCError   `json:"error,omitempty"`
}
```

## Implementation Notes

- **Connection pooling**: Reuse HTTP connections; Agent Mail may be called frequently
- **Timeout handling**: 5s default for most operations, 30s for search/summarize
- **Retry logic**: Exponential backoff for transient failures (503, timeouts)
- **Bearer token**: Read from NTM config or AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN env var
- **Graceful degradation**: All methods return (result, error); callers handle missing server

 {"id":"ntm-gr5b","title":"Implement CASS topic filtering","description":"Filter CASS results by topic/category to inject only relevant context.

PROBLEM:
Not all historical context is helpful. Auth-related history shouldn't be injected for UI tasks.

TOPIC DETECTION:
1. Analyze prompt keywords to detect topic:
   - Auth: "auth", "login", "password", "jwt", "session"
   - UI: "ui", "component", "css", "style", "button"
   - API: "endpoint", "route", "request", "response"
   - Database: "query", "schema", "migration", "model"
   - Testing: "test", "spec", "mock", "fixture"

2. CASS results tagged with topics (from CASS search metadata)

FILTERING RULES:
```toml
[cass.topic_filter]
enabled = true

# Only inject results matching prompt topics
match_topics = true

# Explicit exclusions
exclude_topics = []  # e.g., ["infrastructure", "deployment"]

# Topic weights (boost/penalize)
[cass.topic_weights]
same_topic = 1.5      # boost matching topics
different_topic = 0.5  # penalize mismatched
```

IMPLEMENTATION:
```
prompt_topics = detect_topics(prompt)
for each cass_result:
    result_topics = result.tags or detect_topics(result.summary)
    
    IF exclude_topics INTERSECT result_topics:
        SKIP result
    
    overlap = prompt_topics INTERSECT result_topics
    IF match_topics AND overlap IS EMPTY:
        relevance *= different_topic_weight
    ELSE:
        relevance *= same_topic_weight
```

- Relevance filtering (ntm-mpw1)

-{"id":"ntm-gy4o","title":"Feature: Approval Workflow Engine","description":"# Approval Workflow Engine (internal/approval/)

## Why This Is Critical
Multiple features need approval workflows:
- File Reservation: Force-release of stale reservations
- SLB Safety Gates: Two-person rule for dangerous operations
- BD Daemon: Auto-push (if ever enabled)
- Future: Database migrations, production deployments

Without a unified engine, each feature reimplements approval logic.

## Approval Flow
```
Request → Pending → [Approved | Denied | Expired]
           ↓
      Notification to approver(s)
           ↓
      Approver action (CLI, dashboard, Agent Mail)
           ↓
      Audit log entry
```

## Implementation (internal/approval/engine.go)

### Core Types
```go
type ApprovalEngine struct {
    store      *state.Store
    policy     *policy.Engine
    notifier   *notify.Notifier
    eventBus   *events.Bus
}

type ApprovalRequest struct {
    ID            string        `json:"id"`
    Action        string        `json:"action"`      // "force_release", "push_force"
    Resource      string        `json:"resource"`    // What's being acted on
    Reason        string        `json:"reason"`      // Why approval needed
    RequestedBy   string        `json:"requested_by"` // Agent or user
    CorrelationID string        `json:"correlation_id"`
    RequiresSLB   bool          `json:"requires_slb"` // Two-person rule?
    CreatedAt     time.Time     `json:"created_at"`
    ExpiresAt     time.Time     `json:"expires_at"`
    Status        ApprovalStatus `json:"status"`
    ApprovedBy    string        `json:"approved_by,omitempty"`
    ApprovedAt    *time.Time    `json:"approved_at,omitempty"`
    DeniedReason  string        `json:"denied_reason,omitempty"`
}

type ApprovalStatus string
const (
    StatusPending  ApprovalStatus = "pending"
    StatusApproved ApprovalStatus = "approved"
    StatusDenied   ApprovalStatus = "denied"
    StatusExpired  ApprovalStatus = "expired"
)
```

### Key Methods
```go
// Request creates a new approval request
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Request(ctx context.Context, req ApprovalRequest) (*ApprovalRequest, error)

// Check returns current status of an approval
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Check(ctx context.Context, id string) (*ApprovalRequest, error)

// Approve grants an approval (checks SLB rules)
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Approve(ctx context.Context, id string, approverID string) error

// Deny rejects an approval with reason
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Deny(ctx context.Context, id string, approverID string, reason string) error

// WaitForApproval blocks until approved, denied, or expired
func (e *ApprovalEngine) WaitForApproval(ctx context.Context, id string, timeout time.Duration) (*ApprovalRequest, error)

// ListPending returns all pending approvals
func (e *ApprovalEngine) ListPending(ctx context.Context) ([]ApprovalRequest, error)

// ExpireStale marks old pending approvals as expired
func (e *ApprovalEngine) ExpireStale(ctx context.Context) (int, error)
```

### SLB Two-Person Rule
When `RequiresSLB=true`:
1. Approver cannot be the same as requester
2. Approver must be on the approved list (from policy or config)
3. Extra audit logging

### Notification Integration
```go
func (e *ApprovalEngine) notifyApprovers(req *ApprovalRequest) {
    // Get approver list from config
    approvers := e.config.GetApprovers(req.Action)
    
    // Send via Agent Mail to "Human" or specific approvers
    for _, approver := range approvers {
        e.notifier.Send(ctx, notify.Message{
            To:      approver,
            Subject: fmt.Sprintf("Approval needed: %s", req.Action),
            Body:    formatApprovalRequest(req),
            Urgency: "high",
        })
    }
}
```

### State Store Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE approvals (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    resource TEXT NOT NULL,
    reason TEXT,
    requested_by TEXT NOT NULL,
    correlation_id TEXT,
    requires_slb BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    approved_by TEXT,
    approved_at TIMESTAMP,
    denied_reason TEXT
);
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm approve list              # List pending approvals
ntm approve show <id>         # Show approval details
ntm approve <id>              # Approve a request
ntm approve deny <id> --reason="Too risky"
ntm approve history           # Show past approvals
```

## Event Types
- approval.requested
- approval.approved
- approval.denied
- approval.expired

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Request creates pending approval
- [ ] Check returns current status
- [ ] Approve/Deny work correctly
- [ ] SLB two-person rule enforced
- [ ] Notifications sent to approvers

+{"id":"ntm-gy4o","title":"Feature: Approval Workflow Engine","description":"# Approval Workflow Engine (internal/approval/)

## Why This Is Critical
Multiple features need approval workflows:
- File Reservation: Force-release of stale reservations
- SLB Safety Gates: Two-person rule for dangerous operations
- BD Daemon: Auto-push (if ever enabled)
- Future: Database migrations, production deployments

Without a unified engine, each feature reimplements approval logic.

## Approval Flow
```
Request → Pending → [Approved | Denied | Expired]
           ↓
      Notification to approver(s)
           ↓
      Approver action (CLI, dashboard, Agent Mail)
           ↓
      Audit log entry
```

## Implementation (internal/approval/engine.go)

### Core Types
```go
type ApprovalEngine struct {
    store      *state.Store
    policy     *policy.Engine
    notifier   *notify.Notifier
    eventBus   *events.Bus
}

type ApprovalRequest struct {
    ID            string        `json:"id"`
    Action        string        `json:"action"`      // "force_release", "push_force"
    Resource      string        `json:"resource"`    // What's being acted on
    Reason        string        `json:"reason"`      // Why approval needed
    RequestedBy   string        `json:"requested_by"` // Agent or user
    CorrelationID string        `json:"correlation_id"`
    RequiresSLB   bool          `json:"requires_slb"` // Two-person rule?
    CreatedAt     time.Time     `json:"created_at"`
    ExpiresAt     time.Time     `json:"expires_at"`
    Status        ApprovalStatus `json:"status"`
    ApprovedBy    string        `json:"approved_by,omitempty"`
    ApprovedAt    *time.Time    `json:"approved_at,omitempty"`
    DeniedReason  string        `json:"denied_reason,omitempty"`
}

type ApprovalStatus string
const (
    StatusPending  ApprovalStatus = "pending"
    StatusApproved ApprovalStatus = "approved"
    StatusDenied   ApprovalStatus = "denied"
    StatusExpired  ApprovalStatus = "expired"
)
```

### Key Methods
```go
// Request creates a new approval request
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Request(ctx context.Context, req ApprovalRequest) (*ApprovalRequest, error)

// Check returns current status of an approval
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Check(ctx context.Context, id string) (*ApprovalRequest, error)

// Approve grants an approval (checks SLB rules)
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Approve(ctx context.Context, id string, approverID string) error

// Deny rejects an approval with reason
func (e *ApprovalEngine) Deny(ctx context.Context, id string, approverID string, reason string) error

// WaitForApproval blocks until approved, denied, or expired
func (e *ApprovalEngine) WaitForApproval(ctx context.Context, id string, timeout time.Duration) (*ApprovalRequest, error)

// ListPending returns all pending approvals
func (e *ApprovalEngine) ListPending(ctx context.Context) ([]ApprovalRequest, error)

// ExpireStale marks old pending approvals as expired
func (e *ApprovalEngine) ExpireStale(ctx context.Context) (int, error)
```

### SLB Two-Person Rule
When `RequiresSLB=true`:
1. Approver cannot be the same as requester
2. Approver must be on the approved list (from policy or config)
3. Extra audit logging

### Notification Integration
```go
func (e *ApprovalEngine) notifyApprovers(req *ApprovalRequest) {
    // Get approver list from config
    approvers := e.config.GetApprovers(req.Action)
    
    // Send via Agent Mail to "Human" or specific approvers
    for _, approver := range approvers {
        e.notifier.Send(ctx, notify.Message{
            To:      approver,
            Subject: fmt.Sprintf("Approval needed: %s", req.Action),
            Body:    formatApprovalRequest(req),
            Urgency: "high",
        })
    }
}
```

### State Store Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE approvals (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    action TEXT NOT NULL,
    resource TEXT NOT NULL,
    reason TEXT,
    requested_by TEXT NOT NULL,
    correlation_id TEXT,
    requires_slb BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    approved_by TEXT,
    approved_at TIMESTAMP,
    denied_reason TEXT
);
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm approve list              # List pending approvals
ntm approve show <id>         # Show approval details
ntm approve <id>              # Approve a request
ntm approve deny <id> --reason="Too risky"
ntm approve history           # Show past approvals
```

## Event Types
- approval.requested
- approval.approved
- approval.denied
- approval.expired

- [ ] Expiration handled

 {"id":"ntm-s7mx","title":"Implement auto-checkpointing","description":"## Overview
Automatically create checkpoints at intervals and on key events.

## Location
Create `internal/checkpoint/auto.go`

## Configuration
```go
type AutoCheckpointConfig struct {
    Enabled         bool `toml:"enabled"`
    IntervalMinutes int  `toml:"interval_minutes"`
    MaxCheckpoints  int  `toml:"max_checkpoints"`
    OnRotation      bool `toml:"on_rotation"`
    OnError         bool `toml:"on_error"`
}
```

## Auto-Checkpoint Triggers
1. **Interval**: Every N minutes
2. **Pre-rotation**: Before context window rotation
3. **On error**: When agent error detected
4. **On demand**: Manual trigger via dashboard

## Background Worker
```go
func (a *AutoCheckpointer) Start(ctx context.Context, session string) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(a.config.IntervalMinutes) * time.Minute)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            if err := a.createCheckpoint(session, "auto"); err != nil {
                log.Error("Auto checkpoint failed: %v", err)
            }
            a.pruneOldCheckpoints(session)
            
        case event := <-a.events:
            if event.Type == EventRotation && a.config.OnRotation {
                a.createCheckpoint(session, "pre-rotation")
            }
            
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        }
    }
}
```

## Checkpoint Pruning
- Keep max N checkpoints per session
- Delete oldest first
- Never delete named checkpoints
- Keep at least one per day

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interval-based auto-checkpoint
- [ ] Pre-rotation checkpoint
- [ ] Configurable settings
- [ ] Checkpoint pruning
- [ ] Background worker management
- [ ] Tests for auto-checkpoint logic","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:14:42.55259838-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:14:42.55259838-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-s7mx","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ox6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:14:59.11704009-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-s7mx","depends_on_id":"ntm-59ce","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:15:01.159973248-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-s7mx","depends_on_id":"ntm-cj0w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:17:51.125522394-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-sc6","title":"Implement agent progress detection","description":"Detect and report agent progress through work items.

## Rationale
When an agent is working on a task, I need to understand progress:
- Is it just starting?
- Is it midway through?
- Is it finishing up?
- Has it encountered a blocker?

This enables smarter orchestration (don't interrupt agents near completion, reassign stuck work, etc.)

## Progress Indicators
Detect progress from pane output patterns:

### Starting Indicators
- 'Let me', 'I'll', 'Starting'
- File reads, searches
- Planning/analysis output

### Working Indicators
- File edits
- Code blocks being written
- Test runs
- Build commands

### Finishing Indicators
- 'Done', 'Complete', 'Finished'
- Summary output
- Return to prompt
- Commit messages

### Stuck Indicators
- 'Error', 'Failed', 'Cannot'
- Repeated attempts
- Questions asked
- Long pauses

## Output in Snapshot
```json
{
  "agents": [{
    "pane": "0.1",
    "progress": {
      "stage": "working",     // starting, working, finishing, stuck
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "indicators": ["file_edits", "code_blocks"],
      "time_in_stage_sec": 120
    }
  }]
}
```

## Future: ML-based Detection
Could train a classifier on agent outputs to detect progress more accurately. For now, heuristic-based is sufficient.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:19.308214-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:27:00.311766-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:27:00.311766-05:00"}

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

```json

  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",

    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

 {"id":"ntm-sk07","title":"Feature: CASS Historical Context Injection","description":"# CASS Historical Context Injection

## The Opportunity
CASS indexes 50K+ sessions across 11 different agent types (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Roo, Cline, Windsurf, etc.) with sub-60ms search.

NTM could inject relevant historical context before spawning agents, so they don't reinvent solutions.

## CASS Capabilities
- Multi-agent indexing (11 agent types)
- Full-text search
- Semantic search (embedding-based)
- Hybrid search (combined)
- Multi-machine unified index

## Implementation (internal/context/historical.go)

### Search Function
```go
type CASSMatch struct {
    SessionID   string
    AgentType   string
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Score       float64
    Snippet     string
```

## Why This Is Critical
Multiple features send messages to "Human" (a reserved Agent Mail identity):
- Session Coordinator: Periodic digests
- Approval Workflow: Pending approval requests
- UBS: Critical bug notifications
- Alerts: System alerts

But there's no configuration for HOW humans actually receive these notifications.

## Notification Channels
Humans can receive notifications via:
1. **Desktop notifications** (system native)
2. **Slack** (webhook or app)
3. **Email** (SMTP)
4. **Telegram** (bot API)
5. **File drop** (.ntm/human_inbox/ for offline review)
6. **Agent Mail** (if human has an agent identity)

## Configuration (.ntm/config.yaml)
```yaml
notifications:
  human:
    # Primary channel
    primary: slack
    
    # Fallback if primary fails
    fallback: file
    
    # Channel configurations
    channels:
      slack:
        webhook_url: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}
        channel: "#ntm-alerts"
        
      email:
        smtp_host: smtp.gmail.com
        smtp_port: 587
        from: ntm@example.com
        to: developer@example.com
        
      telegram:
        bot_token: ${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}
        chat_id: ${TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID}
        
      desktop:
        enabled: true
        sound: true
        
      file:
        path: .ntm/human_inbox/
        
    # Routing rules by notification type
    routing:
      digest: [file]           # Low urgency - just log it
      approval: [slack, desktop]  # High urgency - multiple channels
      alert: [slack, desktop, telegram]  # Critical - all channels
      bug: [slack]             # Medium urgency
```

## Implementation (internal/notify/router.go)

### Core Types
```go
type Notifier struct {
    config   *NotifyConfig
    channels map[string]Channel
}

type Channel interface {
    Name() string
    Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error
    Available() bool
}

type Message struct {
    Type       string    // "digest", "approval", "alert", "bug"
    Subject    string
    Body       string
    Urgency    string    // "low", "normal", "high", "critical"
    Metadata   map[string]string
    CreatedAt  time.Time
}

// Concrete implementations
type SlackChannel struct { ... }
type EmailChannel struct { ... }
type TelegramChannel struct { ... }
type DesktopChannel struct { ... }
type FileChannel struct { ... }
```

### Routing Logic
```go
func (n *Notifier) Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error {
    // 1. Get channels for this message type
    channels := n.config.GetChannels(msg.Type)
    if len(channels) == 0 {
        channels = []string{n.config.Primary}
    }
    
    // 2. Try each channel in order
    var lastErr error
    for _, chName := range channels {
        ch := n.channels[chName]
        if ch == nil || !ch.Available() {
            continue
        }
        
        if err := ch.Send(ctx, msg); err != nil {
            lastErr = err
            continue
        }
        
        // Success on at least one channel
        return nil
    }
    
    // 3. Fallback
    if lastErr != nil && n.config.Fallback != "" {
        if fb := n.channels[n.config.Fallback]; fb != nil {
            return fb.Send(ctx, msg)
        }
    }
    
    return lastErr
}
```

### Desktop Notifications
Uses OS-native notification system:
```go
func (d *DesktopChannel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error {
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "darwin":
        // osascript -e 'display notification ...'
        return exec.Command("osascript", "-e",
            fmt.Sprintf(`display notification "%s" with title "%s"`,
                msg.Body, msg.Subject)).Run()
    case "linux":
        // notify-send
        return exec.Command("notify-send", msg.Subject, msg.Body).Run()
    }
    return nil
}
```

### File Channel
Creates markdown files for offline review:
```go
func (f *FileChannel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) error {
    filename := fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s.md", 
        msg.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05"),
        msg.Type)
    path := filepath.Join(f.path, filename)
    
    content := fmt.Sprintf("# %s\n\n%s\n\n---\nUrgency: %s\nTime: %s\n",
        msg.Subject, msg.Body, msg.Urgency, msg.CreatedAt)
    
    return os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644)
}
```

- Session Coordinator: Uses for digest summaries
- Approval Workflow: Uses for pending approvals
- UBS: Uses for bug notifications

+// =============================================================================
+// Rate Limit Backoff (ntm-1ir2)
+// =============================================================================
+//
+// Implements exponential backoff for rate-limited agents:
+// - Base: 30 seconds
+// - Multiplier: 2^n (where n = consecutive rate limits)
+// - Max: 5 minutes
+// - Clears after backoff expires and agent is healthy
+
+const (
+	// BackoffBase is the initial backoff duration
+	BackoffBase = 30 * time.Second
+	// BackoffMax is the maximum backoff duration
+	BackoffMax = 5 * time.Minute
+	// BackoffMultiplier is the exponential multiplier
+	BackoffMultiplier = 2
+)
+
+// RateLimitBackoff tracks backoff state for a single agent
+type RateLimitBackoff struct {
+	PaneID           string    `json:"pane_id"`
+	BackoffCount     int       `json:"backoff_count"`      // n in 30s * 2^n
+	BackoffEndsAt    time.Time `json:"backoff_ends_at"`
+	LastRateLimitAt  time.Time `json:"last_rate_limit_at"`
+	TotalRateLimits  int       `json:"total_rate_limits"`  // lifetime count
+}
+
+// BackoffManager manages rate limit backoffs for all agents in a session
+type BackoffManager struct {
+	mu       sync.RWMutex
+	backoffs map[string]*RateLimitBackoff // keyed by pane ID
+	session  string
+}
+
+// NewBackoffManager creates a new backoff manager for a session
+func NewBackoffManager(session string) *BackoffManager {
+	return &BackoffManager{
+		backoffs: make(map[string]*RateLimitBackoff),
+		session:  session,
+	}
+}
+
+// RecordRateLimit records a rate limit hit and starts/extends backoff
+func (bm *BackoffManager) RecordRateLimit(paneID string) time.Duration {
+	bm.mu.Lock()
+	defer bm.mu.Unlock()
+
+	now := time.Now()
+	backoff, exists := bm.backoffs[paneID]
+
+	if !exists {
+		backoff = &RateLimitBackoff{
+			PaneID:          paneID,
+			BackoffCount:    0,
+			TotalRateLimits: 0,
+		}
+		bm.backoffs[paneID] = backoff
+	}
+
+	// If already in backoff, increment the count (escalate)
+	if now.Before(backoff.BackoffEndsAt) {
+		backoff.BackoffCount++
+	} else {
+		// First rate limit or backoff expired - start fresh
+		backoff.BackoffCount = 0
+	}
+
+	backoff.LastRateLimitAt = now
+	backoff.TotalRateLimits++
+
+	// Calculate backoff duration: 30s * 2^n, capped at 5 minutes
+	duration := calculateBackoffDuration(backoff.BackoffCount)
+	backoff.BackoffEndsAt = now.Add(duration)
+
+	return duration
+}
+
+// calculateBackoffDuration calculates the backoff duration for a given count
+func calculateBackoffDuration(count int) time.Duration {
+	// 30s * 2^n
+	duration := BackoffBase
+	for i := 0; i < count; i++ {
+		duration *= BackoffMultiplier
+		if duration > BackoffMax {
+			return BackoffMax
+		}
+	}
+	return duration
+}
+
+// IsInBackoff returns true if the agent is currently in backoff
+func (bm *BackoffManager) IsInBackoff(paneID string) bool {
+	bm.mu.RLock()
+	defer bm.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	backoff, exists := bm.backoffs[paneID]
+	if !exists {
+		return false
+	}
+
+	return time.Now().Before(backoff.BackoffEndsAt)
+}
+
+// GetBackoffRemaining returns the time remaining in backoff (0 if not in backoff)
+func (bm *BackoffManager) GetBackoffRemaining(paneID string) time.Duration {
+	bm.mu.RLock()
+	defer bm.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	backoff, exists := bm.backoffs[paneID]
+	if !exists {
+		return 0
+	}
+
+	remaining := time.Until(backoff.BackoffEndsAt)
+	if remaining < 0 {
+		return 0
+	}
+	return remaining
+}
+
+// GetBackoff returns the backoff state for an agent (nil if none)
+func (bm *BackoffManager) GetBackoff(paneID string) *RateLimitBackoff {
+	bm.mu.RLock()
+	defer bm.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	backoff, exists := bm.backoffs[paneID]
+	if !exists {
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	// Return a copy
+	copy := *backoff
+	return &copy
+}
+
+// ClearBackoff clears the backoff state for an agent (e.g., after recovery)
+func (bm *BackoffManager) ClearBackoff(paneID string) {
+	bm.mu.Lock()
+	defer bm.mu.Unlock()
+
+	delete(bm.backoffs, paneID)
+}
+
+// ClearExpiredBackoffs removes all expired backoffs
+func (bm *BackoffManager) ClearExpiredBackoffs() int {
+	bm.mu.Lock()
+	defer bm.mu.Unlock()
+
+	now := time.Now()
+	cleared := 0
+
+	for paneID, backoff := range bm.backoffs {
+		if now.After(backoff.BackoffEndsAt) {
+			delete(bm.backoffs, paneID)
+			cleared++
+		}
+	}
+
+	return cleared
+}
+
+// GetAllBackoffs returns all active backoffs
+func (bm *BackoffManager) GetAllBackoffs() map[string]*RateLimitBackoff {
+	bm.mu.RLock()
+	defer bm.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	now := time.Now()
+	result := make(map[string]*RateLimitBackoff)
+
+	for paneID, backoff := range bm.backoffs {
+		if now.Before(backoff.BackoffEndsAt) {
+			copy := *backoff
+			result[paneID] = &copy
+		}
+	}
+
+	return result
+}
+
+// GetRateLimitCount returns the total rate limit count for an agent
+func (bm *BackoffManager) GetRateLimitCount(paneID string) int {
+	bm.mu.RLock()
+	defer bm.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	backoff, exists := bm.backoffs[paneID]
+	if !exists {
+		return 0
+	}
+
+	return backoff.TotalRateLimits
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Global Backoff Manager Registry
+// =============================================================================
+
+var (
+	backoffManagerRegistry   = make(map[string]*BackoffManager)
+	backoffManagerRegistryMu sync.RWMutex
+)
+
+// GetBackoffManager returns the backoff manager for a session, creating if needed
+func GetBackoffManager(session string) *BackoffManager {
+	backoffManagerRegistryMu.RLock()
+	manager, ok := backoffManagerRegistry[session]
+	backoffManagerRegistryMu.RUnlock()
+
+	if ok {
+		return manager
+	}
+
+	backoffManagerRegistryMu.Lock()
+	defer backoffManagerRegistryMu.Unlock()
+
+	// Double-check after acquiring write lock
+	if manager, ok = backoffManagerRegistry[session]; ok {
+		return manager
+	}
+
+	manager = NewBackoffManager(session)
+	backoffManagerRegistry[session] = manager
+	return manager
+}
+
+// ClearBackoffManager removes the backoff manager for a session
+func ClearBackoffManager(session string) {
+	backoffManagerRegistryMu.Lock()
+	defer backoffManagerRegistryMu.Unlock()
+	delete(backoffManagerRegistry, session)
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Integration: Check Rate Limit Before Send
+// =============================================================================
+
+// CheckSendAllowed checks if sending to an agent is allowed (not in backoff)
+// Returns: allowed, backoffRemaining, rateLimitCount
+func CheckSendAllowed(session, paneID string) (bool, time.Duration, int) {
+	manager := GetBackoffManager(session)
+
+	remaining := manager.GetBackoffRemaining(paneID)
+	count := manager.GetRateLimitCount(paneID)
+
+	return remaining == 0, remaining, count
+}
+
+// RecordAgentRateLimit records a rate limit for an agent and returns backoff duration
+func RecordAgentRateLimit(session, paneID string) time.Duration {
+	manager := GetBackoffManager(session)
+	return manager.RecordRateLimit(paneID)
+}
+
+// ClearAgentBackoff clears backoff state for an agent after recovery
+func ClearAgentBackoff(session, paneID string) {
+	manager := GetBackoffManager(session)
+	manager.ClearBackoff(paneID)
+}
